items.title: Emotional Flashbacks: Re-experiencing Trauma States
items.excerpt: Exploring the nature of emotional flashbacks and their distinction from visual flashbacks.
items.psychological_breakdown: Emotional flashbacks involve the sudden, involuntary re-experiencing of emotional states associated with past trauma. Unlike visual flashbacks, they may not include visual imagery but are characterized by intense emotional and physiological responses.

items.title: Hypervigilance: Constant Threat Detection
items.excerpt: An exploration of hypervigilance as a trauma response and its impact on daily functioning.
items.psychological_breakdown: Hypervigilance represents an elevated state of sensory sensitivity and threat detection. The nervous system remains in a state of heightened alert, scanning the environment for potential dangers.

items.title: Trauma Bonding: Intense Connections Through Shared Pain
items.excerpt: Understanding the psychological mechanisms behind trauma bonding and its implications for relationships.
items.psychological_breakdown: Trauma bonding occurs when individuals who have experienced shared traumatic events form intense emotional attachments. The shared experience of danger and survival creates powerful neurochemical bonds.

items.title: Ember Season: A Memoir of Resilience
items.excerpt: A personal narrative exploring the journey through trauma and the slow process of rebuilding.
